prerequisites: A Microsoft Dataverse environment with its environment URL (for example https://yourorg.crm.dynamics.com), supplied in the url config field.
An application registered in your Microsoft Entra ID (Azure AD) tenant, which provides the client_id (Application ID) and tenant_id (Directory ID).
A client secret configured on that app registration under Certificates & secrets; copy the secret value immediately because it is shown only once and use it as client_secret_value.
A Dataverse application user bound to the registered app and assigned a custom security role granting the table read privileges you need.

authMethods.name: Microsoft Entra ID OAuth 2.0 (server-to-server / client credentials)
authMethods.summary: Authenticates as an app using the registered Entra ID application. Supply client_id (Application ID), client_secret_value (the app registration client secret), and tenant_id (Directory ID). Dataverse uses these to acquire an OAuth 2.0 token with the confidential-client scope <environment-url>/.default and calls the Web API as the bound application user. No interactive user or paid license is required.

setupSteps.title: Register an application in Microsoft Entra ID
setupSteps.body: In the Azure portal, register an app in your Entra ID tenant. From the app's Overview, record the Application (client) ID for client_id and the Directory (tenant) ID for tenant_id.

setupSteps.title: Create a client secret
setupSteps.body: In the app registration under Manage > Certificates & secrets, add a client secret with an expiration period. After saving, copy the displayed secret value immediately and use it as client_secret_value; it is not retrievable after you leave the page.

setupSteps.title: Create a Dataverse application user bound to the app
setupSteps.body: In the Power Platform admin center, create an application user in your environment bound to the registered app, and assign it a custom security role that grants read access to the tables you want to sync. For server-to-server access the Access Dynamics 365 as organization users permission is not required.

setupSteps.title: Provide the environment URL and connection settings
setupSteps.body: Set url to your Dataverse environment URL (for example https://yourorg.crm.dynamics.com). The connector calls the Web API at <url>/api/data/v9.2/, which implements OData v4. Optionally set odata_maxpagesize to control the page size requested via the OData Prefer: odata.maxpagesize header.
